execution: Direct via Shopify Admin MCP (graphql_mutation metafieldDefinitionUpdate) — added a `choices` validator to the custom.firmness PRODUCT metafield definition, covering all 8 values currently in use across the active catalog.
catalog_audit_method: Queried all 230 active products' custom.firmness metafield via Shopify Admin MCP graphql_query, paginated in batches of 25-50. Deduped unique values.

current_usage_count.Soft: ~6 products (Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt Soft, LuxeBreeze Soft, Stearns & Foster Estate Soft / Reserve Soft Pillow Top)
current_usage_count.Plush: ~40 products (Diamond Dreamstage 2.0, Stearns & Foster Estate/Lux Plush, Eastman House Royal Sands Plush, Eclipse Glacier Plush, etc.)
current_usage_count.Medium: ~40 products (Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt Medium, Stearns & Foster Estate Medium, Diamond Dreamstage Medium, etc.)
current_usage_count.Firm: ~50 products (Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt/LuxeBreeze Firm, Stearns & Foster Estate Firm, Diamond Rock Extra Firm, Eclipse Cares Ease Firm, etc.)
current_usage_count.Extra Firm: ~5 products (Diamond Rock Extra Firm, Micah Extra Firm, Cobalt Extra Firm, Spring Air Blair/Kaylee Extra Firm)
current_usage_count.Ultra Firm: 2 products (Stearns & Foster Lux Estate Firm/Estate Ultra Firm)

what_the_validator_does: From now on, any productUpdate or metafieldsSet that tries to write a value to custom.firmness outside this 8-value list will be rejected by Shopify with a userErrors entry. Existing data with values in this list continues to work; if any existing product had a value OUTSIDE the list, the value remains stored but cannot be re-saved without changing to a valid choice. The audit confirmed all current values match the choices list, so no existing data is invalidated.

rejected_alternatives.name: Apply the audit's original 4-value list (Soft, Medium, Firm, Extra Firm)
rejected_alternatives.reason: Would have invalidated 43+ products currently using Plush, Medium-Plush, Medium-Firm, or Ultra Firm. The 'almost did this' caught in the prior session was exactly this risk.

rejected_alternatives.name: Apply a stricter 5-value list (Soft, Medium, Plush, Firm, Extra Firm) with normalization of Medium-Plush/Medium-Firm/Ultra Firm
rejected_alternatives.reason: Would require a 3-product data migration first (renaming Medium-Plush → Plush, Medium-Firm → Firm, Ultra Firm → Extra Firm). Doable but loses semantic precision. The merchant uses Medium-Plush and Medium-Firm intentionally to differentiate sibling models — collapsing them would weaken the catalog.

comfort_level_left_alone.reason: custom.comfort_level (the OTHER firmness-adjacent metafield) has 10+ unique values including non-firmness strings: 'Soft Comfort' (~5 products, Malouf sheets — repurposed for fabric description), 'Euro Top Comfort' (~3 products, Chattam & Wells Kingston Clearance — repurposed for top-style description), 'Ultra Plush', 'Medium Soft', 'Medium Firm' (note: space vs hyphen inconsistency with custom.firmness).
comfort_level_left_alone.additional_constraint: PLP filters in app/_components/plp-filters/filters.ts key off custom.comfort_level directly (productMetafield filter). Changing the canonical set requires coordination with the filter UI and Search & Discovery facets.
comfort_level_left_alone.recommendation: Separate sweep needed: (a) normalize comfort_level values (hyphens vs spaces, decide on 'Medium-Soft' vs 'Medium Soft'), (b) move the non-firmness values like 'Soft Comfort' / 'Euro Top Comfort' to a different metafield (custom.comfort_layer or similar), (c) align comfort_level canonical list with custom.firmness, (d) apply choices validator on comfort_level. This is the bigger audit doc §4 (F1) action — requires merchant sign-off because it touches PLP filters.

verification: Mutation response confirmed updatedDefinition.validations contains the choices entry with the 8-value JSON. No userErrors. Future writes to custom.firmness with non-matching values will fail at the Shopify GraphQL layer.
